articles.title: Senate GOP is kickstarting budget reconciliation to fund ICE. Here's how that works.
articles.summary: After a historic partial shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security, Congressional Republicans are looking to a budgetary tool called reconciliation which could enable them to fund immigration en

articles.title: Senate Adopts G.O.P. Budget, Defeating Democrats’ Affordability Proposals
articles.summary: Republicans pushed through a budget plan with a $70 billion increase for immigration enforcement after an overnight session in which they beat back Democratic proposals aimed at lowering costs.

articles.title: How TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
articles.summary: Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.

articles.title: World Press Photo announces Photo of the Year 2026
articles.summary: The executive director of World Press Photo said this image shows the inconsolable grief of children losing their father in a place built for justice. It is a stark and necessary record of family sepa

articles.title: Democrats dominate midterm fundraising, but Republicans have a huge cash advantage
articles.summary: The latest campaign finance reports show Democratic enthusiasm in key House and Senate races, but national Republican groups have far more in the bank to potentially spend down the road.

articles.title: Candidates vying for U.N. secretary-general role face questions
articles.summary: Who will replace U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres when he steps down this year? Four candidates field questions from member states this week at U.N. headquarters.

articles.title: Putin's approval rating falls amid state-imposed internet restrictions
articles.summary: In Russia, expanding state restrictions on the Internet are frustrating daily life for millions — and sending President Putin's approval ratings on a rare downward spiral.

articles.title: Istanbul plants over 3 million tulips for annual festival
articles.summary: Istanbul plants millions of tulips every spring, a reminder of the flower's origins far from the Netherlands.

articles.title: After being held up for months, Ukraine set to get $100B loan from EU
articles.summary: Taking advantage of the electoral defeat of Hungary's Viktor Orban, European Union countries have moved to approve a $100 billion loan to Ukraine.

articles.title: Trump administration flies 10-year-old back from Cuba amid custody fight
articles.summary: President Trump's Department of Justice sent a plane this week to Cuba to return a 10-year-old from Utah who is at the center of a custody fight involving the child's gender identity.

articles.title: Navy Secretary John Phelan Is Leaving the Pentagon and the Trump Administration
articles.summary: John Phelan is leaving the Pentagon after months of tension with Pete Hegseth and other Pentagon leaders. The tumult comes as the Navy has been engaged in war with Iran.

articles.title: War in Iran Gives New Fuel to a Tax Debate in Australia
articles.summary: The world’s third-largest exporter of natural gas, Australia has been too lenient in taxing lucrative gas exports, many in the country argue.

articles.title: Combative, Defensive and Occasionally Contrite, Kennedy Walks a Fine Line
articles.summary: In four days of congressional testimony, the health secretary sought to please the White House and his MAHA base at the same time.

articles.title: Chemical leak at a W.Va. plant kills 2 people, sends 30 more to hospitals, officials say
articles.summary: The leak occurred at the Catalyst Refiners plant, a silver recovery business. An emergency management official says workers were preparing to shut down at least part of the facility when the leak occu

articles.title: Pentagon says Navy secretary is leaving, the latest departure of a top defense leader
articles.summary: Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said John Phelan, the Navy's top civilian official, was "departing the administration, effective immediately." Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become acting secretary

articles.title: Tesla's making money. But it's planning to spend an awful lot more
articles.summary: Tesla's profits were up from this time last year. But the company warned investors to prepare for expensive investments in next-generation technology like humanoid robots and AI.

articles.title: How the War Powers Act Could Pressure Trump to End the Iran War
articles.summary: A decades-old law allows the president to wage war without congressional approval for 60 days, then limits his options for continuing. President Trump may seek to get around it.

articles.title: F.B.I. Said to Have Investigated Times Reporter After Article on Patel’s Girlfriend
articles.summary: The bureau said it is not pursuing a case, but the scrutiny is an example of the Trump administration weighing whether to criminalize routine news gathering.

articles.title: RFK Jr. Says His Department Advises All Children to Get Measles Vaccine
articles.summary: Testifying on Capitol Hill, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. continued to back away from his criticism of the measles shot. But he spoke on behalf of his department, not himself.

articles.title: Iran Again Tightens Its Grip on Shipping in the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Traffic in the strait has all but halted as Iran renews its attacks, striking two vessels on Wednesday. More than 300 ships linked to Iran have passed through the strait since the war began.

articles.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
articles.summary: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
